params.description: Vertical picture graph appears on left. Favorite Playground Activities. Swings 4 symbols, Slides 3, Monkey Bars 5, Sandbox 2. Key visible: Each star = 5.
Vertical bar graph appears on right. Same Playground Activities data. Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10. Axis labeled 0 to 30 in 5s. All bars pre-filled.
Data table appears alongside both graphs showing Playground Activities values: Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10.

beats.text: You've read bar graphs before. Here's the same Playground data as a bar graph.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph in reading mode. Favorite Playground Activities. Swings 4 symbols, Slides 3, Monkey Bars 5, Sandbox 2. Key: Each star = 5.
Vertical bar graph showing same data. Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10. Axis 0–30 in 5s.
Data table showing Playground Activities values.

beats.text: In a picture graph, you count by 5s using SYMBOLS: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25.

elements.description: Vertical picture graph in reading mode. Favorite Playground Activities. Swings 4 symbols, Slides 3, Monkey Bars 5, Sandbox 2. Key: Each star = 5.
Vertical bar graph showing same data. Swings 20, Slides 15, Monkey Bars 25, Sandbox 10. Axis 0–30 in 5s.
Data table showing Playground Activities values.

beats.text: In a bar graph, you count by 5s using the numbers written along the side of the graph on the AXIS. Look—5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. Same skip-counting, just written on the side. Both graphs count by 5s. Where do you see the 5s on the bar graph?
Where do you see the 5s on the bar graph?

validator.description: Student selected On the axis, correct

beats.text: The axis shows the skip-counting: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. That's your scale of 5—built right into the side of the graph.
